title: cycnium humifusum
intro: Cycnium humifusum, also known as devil's thorn or devil's fiddle, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It is a parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by tapping into the roots of its host plant, typically a grass or a forage crop. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. Cycnium humifusum has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, and can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use.
